# Transcrição: Your_Voice_Right_Now_Is_Your_HABITUAL_Voice

- **Fonte:** Your_Voice_Right_Now_Is_Your_HABITUAL_Voice
- **Duração:** 02:12
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:12]

This is the biggest misconception that will keep you sounding the same with your voice for the rest of your life. Most of my students believe that the voice they have right now is their natural voice. How do I know this? Because every single time I get my students to play with their voice and I push them to be a little silly and playful and I get them to do things like this with their voice or I get them to do things like this with their voice, they say things to me like, Oh my god, no Vin, don't make me do that. That's not my natural voice, Vin. Listen, the voice you have right now is not your natural voice. It's not. Your natural voice, you lost it when you were two to three years old. The voice you have right now is what's known as your habitual voice. It's a series of habits that you've developed unconsciously based on the people you were inspired by when you were young. For example, if you were inspired by Mum and Mum had a very strong booming voice then you would adopt that behaviour and you would speak like Mum. If you're inspired by your teacher and your teacher has very quiet speaking habits then because you were inspired by your teacher you would adopt those speaking habits. We do this unconsciously based on the people around us growing up, based on who we're inspired by. And you see communication as a skill set. It's often something that people leave to chance and people don't actually consciously think about this. By the time you've hit your early adulthood, it's one of those things you either have or you don't have. And if you have it, well, you move through life with a little more grace. And if you don't have it, it's like you're driving with the brakes on that also squeal. This is not a skill set that is focused on in our formative years in school. And it's not a skill set that our employers often invest in. At the end of the day, sure, you can just wait for the right person to come into your life and influence you in the right way. Or sure, you could wait till your employer invests in you. Or you can start taking responsibility for your own damn success and take action now. If you want to start working on your communication skills, then join me for a free two hour masterclass that I'm running one final time this year. I've put the link in the comments. Click the link to register and I'll help you learn how to amplify the best parts of who you are. See you at the masterclass.
